reason: Fixes meta-260606T0155Z-loop-surface-inject-spawnandcall-chicken-egg. The old code wrote the JSON-RPC initialize message inside the child.stdout.on("data") handler; since the first stdout data from a freshly spawned MCP server is the response to initialize, the send never fired and every session-start probe hit the 10s timeout. Phase 4 of plan 260605 then logged a meta_state_report finding (subtype=mcp-connection) and printed a banner — masking the underlying bug as a transient connection issue. Verified empirically 2026-06-06: before fix, hook output was the failure banner with reason=timeout; after fix, hook output is the success block (49 tools, 8 record types, 6 active findings). The new real-spawn test (loop-surface-inject-real-spawn.test.cjs) locks the end-to-end behavior by racing the probe against a 9s wall clock — the old code would have failed this test at ~9s. Closes Gap #2 (meta-260605T1356Z-sp0-sp3-tools-require-live-mcp-server-connection) empirically: the SessionStart probe now succeeds in real sessions, so the 5 SP0-SP3 tools are reachable via the canonical mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* surface.

reason: Closes the splitSegments-quote-unaware bug (finding meta-260606T0301Z-...). The P1 G8 subcommand-class fix (meta-260606T0225Z-...) shipped a refined rule pattern but assumed splitSegments handled quoted message bodies correctly. Empirically 2026-06-06, a multi-paragraph git commit describing the P1 fix itself (with trigger phrases like 'propose/design/create a new schema, new schema, create schema' inside -m bodies) was still escalated by the rule, because the ';' in 'false positives; CLI subcommand' split the body into 16 segments, and each fragment was checked independently by the regex. The new state machine keeps the entire quoted body as one segment, so stripMessageFlags correctly strips the -m value and the regex sees only 'git commit'. Blast radius: every applyPromotedRules + matchConstraintPattern caller. POSIX shell semantics preserved (single quotes are literal; double quotes allow backslash escapes; backslash outside quotes escapes the next char).

title: Meta-state cross-reference fields — typed fields on rule and loop-design schemas
description:
Schema enhancement design captured for a future plan: add formal cross-reference field for this change-log/finding targets or relates to that finding relationships. Current state (2026-06-06): meta-state.jsonl has no formal related-to or design-target field. The only formal cross-reference fields are (1) consolidated_into (finding -> change-log; valid only with status=superseded), (2) consolidates (change-log -> finding; means these findings are superseded by this change-log), and (3) supersedes (change-log -> change-log). None of these express this design note targets that active finding. Proposed shape (option A): extend change-log applies_to to include findings: applies_to.findings: z.array(z.string()).optional(). Proposed shape (option B): add a generic related_to: z.array(z.string()).optional() to both finding and change-log schemas. Use case: design notes (like the cold-session test rule design captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred, which targets meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list) need to formally reference the target finding they are a design for. Workaround in use today: mention the target finding id in the description field (discoverable via grep, not via schema). Status: design note; deferred for a future plan per operator decision 2026-06-06. Operators and future plans discover this design note via meta_state_list with subtype=meta-state-schema-enhancement.

GAP ADDED 2026-06-08: The original design proposed typed cross-reference fields (proposed_design_for + addresses), but did not include the CRUD operations needed to maintain those fields. The MCP tool surface covers Create, Read, and (partially) Resolve, but lacks Update/Patch on existing entries. Concrete instance: this entry was just updated to add addresses=[the CRUD-coverage finding] via direct I/O (node -e importing core/meta-state.js#updateEntry) because meta_state_propose_design returns already_exists on id collision. The gap recurs in any context where an agent needs to update an existing entry: backfilling code_fingerprint on a resolved finding, updating evidence_code_ref after a :line -> #anchor refactor, refining a rule refinement_reason, etc. The fix must ship as part of this design: meta_state_patch (new tool with CAS) OR meta_state_propose_design update-or-create mode. Once the CRUD operation exists, the cross-reference fields become operationally useful (an agent can update proposed_design_for when the design evolves, can update addresses when a new finding motivates the design) rather than declarative-only (set at creation, frozen).

title: meta_state_patch wire-format recursion: unwrap {item:[...]} + ZodObject passthrough recursion
description: The meta_state_patch MCP tool has two related wire-format bugs that corrupt data when an agent calls it via the MCP stdio transport: (1) top-level array values in patch get wrapped as {item: [...]} when the patch object contains other top-level fields alongside the array, and (2) coerceParamsToSchema does not recurse into ZodObject schemas with .shape missing (the passthrough case used by patch: z.object({}).passthrough()). The cross-reference-fields plan worked around (1) by patching the array alone, then scalars alone. Plan 260609-adopt-instruction-layer hit both bugs in Phase 3 and required an operator-approved node -e escape hatch to unwrap the corrupted value. Proposed fix: (a) extend coerceParamsToSchema to recurse into passthrough ZodObjects AND unwrap {item: [...]} when the target field is a ZodArray; (b) add a regression test file that calls meta_state_patch over the stdio transport with a combined patch (array + scalars) and asserts the stored value is a flat array, not a wrapped object. The structural fix (Bridge 5) is to derive the patch tool schema from the loop-design schema at startup so passthrough goes away. Until then, this design ships the symptom-level fix. Addresses 2 gaps: the meta_state_patch array wrap (newly observed) and the boolean coercion in meta_state_report (related symptom of the same wire-format root cause).

The design is a symptom-level fix: extend coerceParamsToSchema to recurse into passthrough ZodObjects and unwrap {item: [...]} when the target field is a ZodArray; add a regression test for combined-patch calls. The structural fix (Bridge 5) is to derive the patch tool schema from the loop-design schema at startup so passthrough goes away. Filed the design + finding via direct writeEntry because meta_state_propose_design has the same wire-format bug (top-level array fields wrap as {item: [...]}). This is the documented data-integrity fix pattern (operator-approved), not the meta-260606T2102Z anti-pattern.

The meta_state_patch tool was corrupting data when called with combined array + scalar fields via stdio transport (top-level arrays wrapped as {item: [...]}). The fix lives in tool-registry.js#coerceParamsToSchema (the contract layer between wire format and Zod), adds a typeName-gated unwrapItemWrap helper (ZodArray or ZodObject only, max 3 iterations inlined). title: Meta-state refresh-fingerprint loop circuit-breaker (idempotency cache + report auto-default)
description: Two-phase fix for the meta_state_refresh_fingerprint retry-loop pathology. Phase 1: in-handler idempotency cache (Map keyed on (id, previous_code_fingerprint), 60s TTL) collapses repeat calls within the same MCP server process to a single result, with a cache_hit: true flag for observability. Phase 2: auto-default mechanism_check: true when evidence_code_ref is present in meta_state_report (using ?? not ||, so explicit mechanism_check: false is preserved), plus a structured warnings array in the response when the caller passes evidence_code_ref with explicit mechanism_check: false. Both phases together: the auto-default closes the confusing-error half (a finding with evidence_code_ref and mechanism_check: null is unreachable), and the cache caps the blast radius of any model reflex that survives (153 identical calls collapse to 1). Compound fix is required: shipping only the cache leaves a UX bug (silent miss when agents provide evidence_code_ref without mechanism_check); shipping only the auto-default leaves a structural gap (any model reflex that re-runs refresh 100+ times still wastes 53 minutes). Touchpoints: meta-state-refresh-fingerprint-tool.js (~10 lines for the cache + get/set + wire-up + export _clearCacheForTests) and meta-state-report-tool.js (~9 lines: change one spread, add 8 lines for warnings). Tests: 4 cache tests (same key returns cache_hit, different fingerprint is miss, TTL expiry re-runs, error responses also cached) + 5 report tests (default-true when evidence_code_ref provided, no warning on explicit true, warning on explicit false, no field when both absent, escape hatch when only mechanism_check provided). Plan path: plans/260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker/. Brainstorm: plans/reports/brainstorm-260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker.md. Predecessor finding meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-tool-5-times-in-succe resolved by auto-resolve without code fix, leaving the gap open.

title: Session-closeout audit agent (replaces ck:journal)
description: Session-closeout audit agent: before closing a session, an automated agent scans the session transcript + filesystem deltas + tool-call history for any problems that occurred but were not yet filed to meta-state (finding, change-log, or rule). For each unfiled problem, the agent auto-files the appropriate entry kind. Replaces the manual `ck:journal` mechanism (the operator-or-agent writes a journal entry at session end) with a loop-owned enforcement surface that cannot be forgotten. The motivation is the recurring pattern observed across 2026-06-06..2026-06-13: agents (including this one) ship code changes, gate fixes, and tool deletions without filing findings for the bugs they encountered en route; the next session has to rediscover the same problems (4 pre-existing test failures filed 2026-06-13, all of which had been latent for 2+ days; the L2 probe test flakiness pattern recurring 2026-06-10 and again 2026-06-13; the context-pollution-stale-workaround-language pattern where a fixed bug's workaround language persisted in AGENTS.md because the agent applying the fix did not file a follow-up). The design space: (1) hook-based: SessionEnd hook scans the transcript + gate-decision log for unfiled problems, prompts the agent before close, (2) MCP-tool-based: ck:closeout MCP tool that the agent calls before session end, (3) workflow-based: workflow_session_closeout triggered by the orchestrator, (4) ad-hoc convention: the agent is supposed to call meta_state_report on any unfiled problem (current state, often skipped). The recommended path is (1) hook-based: it is the only path that cannot be skipped, and it is the path the loop already uses for SessionStart (loop-surface-inject.cjs, H1-H14 hint injection). The hook would: (a) collect all gate-decision blocks (decision: block, decision: escalate) emitted during the session; (b) collect all TODO/HACK/FIXME/XXX comments added to the diff; (c) collect all untracked files at session end; (d) for each, check meta_state_list for an existing finding/change-log with the same evidence_code_ref; (e) if no match, file a finding with the session as session_id and the gate decision / TODO comment / untracked file as evidence. The agent is then asked to ack-or-amend the auto-filed findings before session close. This unifies the audit trail across all session activities: code changes ship with the change-log (already enforced), but the *problems* the agent encountered en route (gate blocks, TODOs left in code, untracked files that look like scratch work) get captured too. Phase: deferred to after the productization master tracker Phase A-G ships (per the operator's "much later" framing), but worth writing down now because the design space is non-trivial and the failure mode (unfiled problems haunting the next session) is already paying the loop's cognitive-load cost.

description: Import-chain analysis is the canonical dead-code detection method; keyword-based search misses transitive core dependencies. The anti-pattern has recurred 4 times across dead-code cleanup plans: (1) plan 260613-1000 missed core/candidate-to-experiment/{experiment-draft-builder,template-registry}.js + __tests__/bridge-2-unit.test.js; (2) plan 260613-1421 caught (keyword scan worked for that batch); (3) plan 260613-1530 missed core/{schema-description-loader,schema-loader,yaml-parse-wrapper}.js + scout/index.js; (4) this session caught all 7 via grep + reverse import map. Total: 11 stranded files across 2 of 4 cleanup passes. Process fix: when a tool file is deleted, run import-chain analysis on its dependencies (walk all .js files, extract import/require statements, build reverse map, find files with zero live importers excluding entry points) before declaring the cleanup complete. Keyword matching (substring search for the deleted file's basename) is deprecated for this task. The 'meta-260613T1546Z-dead-code-cleanup-process-is-keyword-based-not-import-chain' finding (now resolved) captured the same lesson; this finding elevates it to a meta-pattern. Promote to rule entry so the loop enforces the import-chain step before any tool deletion.

Gate-enforced rule: rule-import-chain-analysis-after-tool-deletion. Pattern type=regex; pattern=rm\s+[^\s]*-tool\.js|rm\s+.*tools/learning-loop-mcp/[^\s]+\.js|git\s+rm\s+[^\s]*-tool\.js. Agent-side consult: when the agent is about to delete a tool file or any .js file under tools/learning-loop-mcp/, the loop reminds the agent to run import-chain analysis (walk all .js files, extract import/require statements, build reverse map, find files with zero live importers) before declaring the cleanup complete. Keyword matching (substring search for the deleted file's basename) is deprecated for this task. Originates from finding meta-260613T1615Z which captured the 4-occurrence meta-pattern (11 stranded files across 2 of 4 cleanup plans 260613-1000, 260613-1421, 260613-1530, this session). The process fix from meta-260613T1546Z-dead-code-cleanup-process-is-keyword-based-not-import-chain is now enforced by the loop. Scope: project_has_learning_loop_mcp.

description:
No MCP path exists to unarchive a meta-state entry or transition out of `status: archived`. The IMMUTABLE_PATCH_FIELDS deny-list in meta-state-patch-tool.js:6-23 blocks any attempt to clear `archived_at` / `archived_by` / `archived_reason` via meta_state_patch. In session 06085a38 (transcript line 465) the recovery from an erroneous rule archival required a direct Python edit of meta-state.jsonl, which bypassed the gate/audit surface, skipped the CAS path through updateEntry, and left `resolved_at`/`resolution` fields on the now-active entry. P0/P1 fixes for the same incident shipped (archive/resolve entry-kind guards, session_id discoverability, bulk-archive preview). The recommended remediation per plans/reports/debugger-260614-0207-session-06085a38-meta-state-process-gaps.md is either (a) extend meta_state_patch to allow archived -> active transitions and auto-clear archived_* fields, or (b) add a dedicated meta_state_unarchive tool. Direct JSONL edits must not remain a recovery path.

[closeout 2026-07-10] KEEP-OPEN (LIVE, reason corrected): verified 2026-07-10 in plan 260710-0104-drift-driven-registry-closeout. grep `unarchive` in tools/learning-loop-mastra/ = 0; no first-class `meta_state_unarchive` tool. Correction: `IMMUTABLE_PATCH_FIELDS` does NOT include `archived_*`/`status` (verified meta-state.js:284-294), so the finding's "IMMUTABLE blocks archived_*" premise is stale; the gap is the missing sanctioned tool, not a patch block. `evidence_test` path `tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/meta-state-archive-tool.test.js` no longer exists (dead reference; directory was moved).

No automated registry-consistency check exists to detect entries whose `status` and audit fields disagree. After the manual restore in session 06085a38, meta-state.jsonl:19 carried `status: active` together with `resolved_at` + `resolution` (Finding 4 of plans/reports/debugger-260614-0207-session-06085a38-meta-state-process-gaps.md). The P0 cleanup removed the visible residue, but a defense-in-depth probe is needed so the next drift is caught at the registry layer, not by an operator. Expected invariants: `status: active` must not carry `resolved_at` / `resolution`; `status: archived` must carry `archived_at` / `archived_by` / `archived_reason`; `status: resolved` must carry `resolved_by`; `status: superseded` must carry `consolidated_into`. Remediation: add a test under tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/ that walks the live registry and fails on any status/audit-field mismatch, OR expose a `meta_state_consistency_check` MCP probe that returns the list of drifted entries without mutating state.

description:
The runtime-agnostic pattern is real in this codebase (shim-not-fork, protocol-adapter, cross-surface iteration) but is NOT codified as a rule. The pattern exists in the code; what's missing is the codified invariant + the surfaces that teach it to future features and audit compliance.

Two existing asymmetries surface the gap:

1. tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/gate-logic.js:407 — GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST = ["product/", "docs/", "plans/", "tools/", ".factory/", "meta-state.jsonl"]. The list includes ".factory/" but NOT ".claude/". A future Claude-targeted glob pattern (e.g., a Claude-coordination equivalent of rule-project-skill-boundary) would be silently dropped by the whitelist check. This is a known asymmetry between the two supported runtimes.

2. tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/inbound-state.js:34-65 — readLastOperatorMessage hard-codes both .claude/coordination/ and .factory/coordination/ paths inline. The cross-surface iteration is duplicated logic; if a third surface is added, the function needs editing in two places (lines 45-54 + 59-69). Same anti-pattern recurs in the bash gate's PATH_WRITE_PATTERNS which lists ".claude/coordination/" and ".factory/coordination/" markers separately (tools/learning-loop-mcp/hooks/bash-gate.js:31-41).

The fix is the layered design captured in plans/reports/brainstorm-260615-1400-runtime-agnostic-features-rule.md. The design authors 6 implementation artifacts:

- AGENTS.md §2 amendment (the design spec; subsection "Runtime-Agnostic Pattern" + 6-item checklist)
- Meta-state rule entry rule-runtime-agnostic-features (the codified invariant; enforcement: agent, pattern_type: consult-checklist, pattern = JSON-serialized 6-item checklist)
- New pattern type consult-checklist in core/gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules (no-op for command-time; recognized for the rule to load; precedent: resolution-evidence-required added 2026-06-06 in meta-260606T1656Z-...)
- New MCP tool check_runtime_agnostic at tools/learning-loop-mcp/tools/check-runtime-agnostic-tool.js (the audit surface; agent-consulted; new agent-manifest group runtime_agnostic)
- core/surfaces.js helper (the cross-surface API; SURFACES = [".claude", ".factory"] is the single source of truth; helpers getAllCoordinationPaths / writeToAllSurfaces / readFromAllSurfaces)
- __tests__/runtime-agnostic.test.js (the regression guard; locks in the existing universal pattern; catches new violations at test time)

Plus 2 refactors that fix the existing asymmetries:
- core/gate-logic.js#GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST — refactor to use surfaces.js#SURFACES; closes the .claude/ asymmetry.
- core/inbound-state.js#readLastOperatorMessage — refactor to use surfaces.js#readFromAllSurfaces; DRY the cross-surface iteration.

The 6-item checklist (the rule's pattern):
1. core-in-universal-location — core logic in tools/learning-loop-mcp/{core,hooks,tools}/, not under .claude/ or .factory/
2. shims-in-sync — if hooks are needed, both .claude/coordination/hooks/ and .factory/coordination/hooks/ have a shim that delegates to the universal hook
3. protocol-adapter-i-o — hook stdin/stdout goes through protocol-adapter.js (parseInput, formatOutput, normalizeToolName)
4. manifest-registered — new MCP tools registered in tools/learning-loop-mcp/agent-manifest.json with group and description
5. cross-surface-iteration — code touching surface-specific paths uses the surfaces.js helper, not hard-coded .claude/ or .factory/ paths
6. parameterized-for-new-surfaces — SURFACES is the single source of truth; adding a new runtime is a 1-line append, not a refactor

Resolution criteria (all 6 must hold):
- AGENTS.md §2 has the "Runtime-Agnostic Pattern" subsection listing the 6 items
- meta-state.jsonl has rule-runtime-agnostic-features active with enforcement: agent, pattern_type: consult-checklist
- core/gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules handles consult-checklist as a no-op (with optional debug warning)
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/tools/check-runtime-agnostic-tool.js exists, registered in agent-manifest.json runtime_agnostic group, returns structured feedback with fix_suggestion
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/surfaces.js exists; SURFACES is the only place runtime names are listed; helpers are tested
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/runtime-agnostic.test.js passes (regression guard; mutation test: adding a hard-coded surface path to core/ fails the test)
- core/gate-logic.js#GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST uses surfaces.js#SURFACES (closes the .claude/ asymmetry)

description: The new stripNodeEvalBody function in tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/gate-logic.js blanks the body of node -e|--eval|-p|--print wrappers before constraint-pattern regex matching. Trade-off: node -e "require('child_process').exec('npm install')" no longer matches the package-manager constraint (the command is inside the blanked body). This is an accepted bypass, not a fix; the user-stated design from plans/reports/brainstorm-260615-1300-bash-gate-debate-friendly-and-string-literal-fix.md#plan-2 chose asymmetry (only node, not python-c/bash-c) because python -c "import docker" IS a real command and the body must remain visible (existing tests at __tests__/gate-logic-quoted-strings.test.js lock this in), and the node -e pattern is rare in real agent flows. Catch-net: the gate_check_recurrence MCP tool shipped in plan 260615-1530-bash-gate-debate-stderr-override-recurrence auto-files a finding via meta_state_report if node -e "..." matches a constraint N>=3 times in M<=10min. The recurrence tracker reads .gate-decision.log and groups by rule_id + command_prefix_normalized. The operator/agent can resolve this finding manually by refining the strip or by promoting a new gate rule that catches node -e wrappers specifically.

description: inbound-gate.js still contains a local TTL-based staleness check (`findStaleObservations`, lines 50-57) that duplicates the semantics of `core/inbound-state.js#checkObservationStaleness`. The old plan 260614-1856-GH-1259-fix-stale-records-references phase 2 step 5 asked to consolidate them, but that plan is completed and the step was never executed. A direct consolidation is blocked by the F1 fix from plan 260517-2300-gate-v2-staleness-fixes: the inbound gate must only write the operator-message marker when observations are actually stale, because `checkObservationStaleness` requires an existing marker to detect staleness. Removing `findStaleObservations` and calling `checkObservationStaleness` would either violate F1 (marker written unconditionally) or prevent the gate from ever bootstrapping (no marker → stale:false forever). Needs a new design/session independent of plan 260614-1856: either a unified staleness primitive that supports both TTL bootstrap and marker-relative semantics, or a change to the F1 invariant. Replaces/supersedes the resolved finding meta-260614T2138Z-plan-260614-1856-gh-1259-fix-stale-records-references-phase, which was tied to the old plan.

description: `meta_state_relationships` graph is unidirectional on `reopens`. The cold-tier pass in `core/loop-introspect.js:285-330` builds the `reopens_inverse` map (parent_id → child_ids[]) and the relationships tool returns `inbound.reopened_by` on the parent. But the same tool returns `outbound: null` on the child when `entry.reopens` is set. Verified 2026-06-23 against `meta-260623T0223Z-...` (reopens=[`meta-260622T1439Z-...`]): `relationships({id: child, direction: outbound})` → `null`; `relationships({id: parent, direction: inbound})` → `{reopened_by: [child]}`. Workaround: query the parent. The two flags produce INCOMPATIBLE JSON; the audit --*-baseline flag fails to parse the regression format."},{"id":"baseline-storage","description":"`fallow` auto-creates `<root>/.fallow/.gitignore: *` that silently gitignores `<root>/.fallow/baselines/`. Verify `git ls-files <root>/.fallow/baselines/` returns expected files BEFORE committing. Prefer `<root>/baselines/fallow/` (committed, outside the auto-ignored cache); the previous `plans/<plan-slug>/reports/fallow/` location is deprecated because it couples CI to a plans directory. Baselines are package-scoped: each fallow-audited package gets its own `<root>/baselines/fallow/`."},{"id":"third-party-action-sha-pin","description":"When swapping a hand-rolled `pnpm exec <tool>` / `npx <tool>` for a third-party GitHub Action (`uses: <vendor>/<tool>@<ref>`), pin to commit SHA, not tag (`uses: <vendor>/<tool>@<sha>`). Verify the Action provides cryptographic signature verification (e.g., fallow-rs/fallow v2 uses Ed25519 + SHA-256 + sentinel at npm/fallow/scripts/{verify-binary,lazy-verify,run-binary}.js) and assert `verified: yes (<sentinel-path>)` in the CI logs. Tag-based pins drift silently when the upstream re-tags; SHA pins survive tag deletion. CLI binary version is pinned separately via `version:` input (or read from `package.json devDependencies.<tool>` via a setup step)."}]}

description: Orchestrator session read the full 326-line source of tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/meta-state-dispatch-finding-tool.js before invoking meta_state_dispatch_finding — pure overhead. The MCP schema description already conveyed everything needed for the invocation: two stages (prepare / commit), two-surfaces split (agent runs gh, not the tool), OPERATOR_MODE gating on commit, all field names + types, and the idempotency contract. The source read added zero invocation-relevant information; only redundant confirmation of the OPERATOR_MODE env-var name (which is in the schema description). Observed in 2026-07-04 dispatch flow: meta-260704T0933Z-local-fallow-gate-cannot-fully-self-verify-and-the-file-inde → issue #34 in dat9uy/learning-loop-template. The full tool-source read consumed ~3.5K tokens of context for zero new signal. Root cause: scout-over-everything reflex. The cook skill HARD-GATE-SCOUT-FIRST is about codebase context (existing patterns, modules, public APIs) — NOT about the tool being invoked. The MCP schema description IS the canonical contract for invocation; the source is for debugging or extending the tool. Suggested rules for orchestrator sessions: (1) treat the MCP tool description/schema as the contract — read it (it's surfaced on call), do not re-read the source; (2) read tool source ONLY when (a) the tool misbehaves and a bug is suspected, (b) you need to extend the tool, or (c) the schema is genuinely insufficient; (3) when scouting, scout the SURROUNDING codebase (similar tools, existing patterns) — not the tool you are about to call; (4) counter-rule: don't over-correct — for novel tools with terse descriptions, a one-line schema glance is fine; full-file reads are not.

The plan-260704-0301 decision to keep dispatch and ack orthogonal (plan.md:144 — "Dispatch is built compatible (explicit resolve, no ack, delegated_to recorded in the ledger)") was correct v1, given that the lifecycle-redesign plan (drop ack/active, collapse to one open state, re-architect OPERATOR_MODE → delegated scoped authority) was deferred. The dispatch feature shipped 2026-07-04 (PR #33) and is now in live use, producing immediate evidence that the deferred lifecycle decision is no longer safely deferrable. Observed in this session: meta-260704T0933Z-local-fallow-gate-cannot-fully-self-verify-and-the-file-inde dispatched to issue #34 in dat9uy/learning-loop-template (commit landed in operator window; ledger_ref patched, runtime-state dispatch row written, issue OPEN); registry entry still shows status=reported with expires_at=2026-07-05T02:33:01.794Z. The TTL clock will age this finding to stale, surface it again in Rec 10 dispatch hints, or auto-resolve — none of which reflect operator intent (the operator already acted by creating the issue + committing the ledger). The dispatch tool itself is correct-by-plan; the inconsistency is between the plan (no ack on dispatch) and operator intent (a dispatched finding is a deferred decision), surfaced by real use on day 1 of production. This is not a fix to apply — it is an evidence artifact that argues for prioritizing the lifecycle-redesign brainstorm over its current deferred slot. The brainstorm must resolve three coupled questions: (1) keep status=active (with explicit ack) and have dispatch drive it; (2) collapse to one open state and have dispatch be a metadata flag instead of a status transition; (3) introduce a third state (e.g., deferred) distinct from both reported and active. Until then, dispatch behavior is correct but inconsistent-with-operator-intent — a documented tension to brainstorm, not a code change to ship.

Close-flow chat bullets are ephemeral instructions, not enforceable rules. Pattern observed 2026-07-04: after verifying the dispatch of meta-260704T0933Z-local-fallow-gate-cannot-fully-self-verify-and-the-file-inde to issue #34 in dat9uy/learning-loop-template, the orchestrator message ended with a "Next step (close flow, when the fix lands)" block enumerating 3 expected actions (merge fix PR → meta_state_refresh_file_index → meta_state_resolve with PR + change-log refs). Those bullets live only in the conversation log — they are NOT surfaced via Rec 10, NOT stored in the registry, NOT gated at meta_state_resolve time. The next agent has no programmatic way to retrieve or be reminded to follow them. The instruction layer for the loop is rule entries (promoted via meta_state_promote_rule) and runtime surfacing (SessionStart hook), NOT chat output. Three candidate mechanisms to mechanize close-flow: (a) resolution-evidence-required rule gating meta_state_resolve for findings with ledger_ref-set, requiring evidence fields {file_index_refreshed_path, change_log_id, pr_ref}; (b) consult-checklist rule firing at session start when a dispatched finding is in the active set, prompting the operator/agent to walk the close-flow before resolving; (c) SessionStart surfacing block listing "open dispatches → close flow pending" alongside the existing Rec 10 stale-dispatch hints. This finding is the evidence argument; the implementation is a separate workstream. description:
Close-flow / finding-triage gap is the symptom; the root cause is architectural — the loop never promoted transport to L1. Transport is named in docs/runtime-contract.md (L2: the 4 transport-agnostic runtime capabilities, with MCP+hooks / library-import / shell-hook-only listed as candidate transports) and realized in docs/architecture.md (L3: the 3-layer architecture hard-codes MCP — "Runtime Interface" IS "the MCP-transport conformance checklist"), but the loop's core meta-state lifecycle operations (report / ack / log_change / resolve / refresh_file_index / dispatch / supersede) are implemented AS MCP tools (L3), not as Core functions (L1) behind a transport adapter. There is no L1 seam between "what the operation is" and "how it is invoked." MCP is de facto foundation because nothing above it abstracts the operation from the transport.

Two failure modes, both symptoms of the missing L1 seam:

(A) WRONG-ROOT MCP (alive but rooted at main): session f3972a8d-ba4e-48ea-a26d-f2152e5d3d99 worked in worktree .worktrees/fix-issue-34-fallow-self-verify/, but .mcp.json lives at the repo root and starts tools/learning-loop-mastra/mastra/server.js with LOOP_SURFACE=.claude rooted at main. The live MCP could not see worktree-only files: meta_state_refresh_file_index({path:"tools/learning-loop-mastra/scripts/gate-self-verify.mjs"}) returned code_missing even though the file existed in the worktree. The operation was unreachable because the operation IS the MCP tool — there is no Core function to call from a shell in the worktree.

(B) DEAD MCP (after pkill): the agent ran pkill -f "mastra/server.js" (exit 144) trying to re-root; every subsequent mcp__learning-loop__mastra_meta_state_* call returned "No such tool available." Restarting from the shell was blocked by the Claude Code auto-mode classifier as Self-Modification ("Starting/restarting the MCP server ... touches the agent's own MCP/runtime infrastructure"); a separate `git push origin main` for the close-flow commit was blocked as Git Push to Default Branch. The agent's own reasoning: "To restart it rooted at the worktree, I would need to change the .mcp.json or restart Claude Code." The only remaining path — the operator manually opening a fresh Claude Code session — is an out-of-band step with no in-loop mechanism.

Cornered-agent bypass attempts (defense-in-depth caught all): Write to file-index.jsonl blocked by the loop write-gate ("Direct writes ... poisoning the index would mask drift"); `node seed-file-index.mjs` blocked by the auto-classifier as Irreversible Local Destruction. These are the agent discovering, under pressure, that there is no non-MCP path to a core operation — a symptom of the missing L1 transport seam.

DEBATE TO OPEN (the unified plan must resolve this, not assume it; the debate spans BOTH surfaces): is MCP the correct transport for the learning loop's core state machine? MCP is an LLM-tool-calling protocol — agent-facing, stdio, a long-lived child process the agent cannot restart (classifier-blocked as self-modification) and cannot re-root to a worktree, and that dies when the shell dies. The loop's core operations are also operator-facing (close-flow after a merge), CI-facing (dispatch on PR event), and shell-facing (worktree work). Coupling audit-trail integrity to MCP couples it to a process with the wrong availability model for an audit trail. runtime-contract.md already names library-import and shell-hook-only transports as forward options; this finding argues they should be evaluated as peer first-class transports now, not deferred. The debate has two surfaces that must both be understood before deciding: (1) CONCEPTUAL (L1) — what transport means as an engine concept, the operation-vs-invocation seam, whether transport belongs in the engine invariant in docs/loop-engine.md, and what 'Core is transport-neutral' commits the loop to; (2) IMPLEMENTATION (L3) — how MCP / CLI / library each realize the 4 runtime capabilities, per-transport restart/re-root semantics, worktree rooting, and gate coverage (does the bash/write-gate see a CLI adapter the same way it sees an MCP tool call?). Promoting transport to L1 means: core meta-state operations are Core functions; a transport interface exposes them; MCP, CLI, and library are peer adapters. The CLI adapter is the immediate unblock (close-flow runs in the shell CWD, sees worktree state, survives MCP death). Whether MCP remains the default transport for audit-trail operations or becomes an agent-convenience adapter over a CLI/library primary is the debate — the evidence above (can't restart, can't re-root, dies with the shell) argues MCP should not be the load-bearing transport for audit-trail integrity.

Compounds with meta-260704T1010Z- (close-flow chat bullets are ephemeral): even with a live MCP, the close-flow contract is not enforced at meta_state_resolve time, so a cornered agent that skips steps leaves orphan findings and a degraded audit trail. The L1-transport promotion and the resolution-evidence gate are complementary: the gate enforces *what* the close-flow must produce; the transport layer determines *whether the agent can produce it*.

Resolution path (unified plan with meta-260704T1010Z-): (i) promote transport to L1 — define core meta-state operations as Core functions behind a transport interface; the doc-location decision (new docs/transport-layer.md vs extending docs/runtime-contract.md) is deferred to the plan and decided on AGENT READABILITY — whichever structure lets an agent locate 'the loop has N peer transports and Core is transport-neutral' in one read; (ii) ship a CLI adapter for log_change / resolve / refresh_file_index / dispatch that invokes Core directly, mirroring gate-self-verify.mjs — runs in the shell CWD, sees worktree state, survives MCP death (immediate unblock); (iii) make plans/reports/close-flow-*.md executable artifacts (Node script + registry-cited block) so `pnpm close-flow:run <finding-id>` works without MCP; (iv) gate meta_state_resolve behind a resolution-evidence-required rule requiring {pr_ref, change_log_id, file_index_refreshed_path}; (v) extend SessionStart stale_dispatch_hints to surface executable close-flow commands alongside open dispatches; (vi) decide the default transport for audit-trail operations (the debate — do not assume MCP stays default); (vii) decide worktree MCP rooting (per-worktree .mcp.json or a CWD-aware server) as a near-term mitigation while the debate is open. Acceptance: a close-flow executes end-to-end with node + shell only (no Claude Code session required); bash/write-gate covers all records/** and file-index.jsonl writes; every core meta-state operation is reachable through at least two transports.

Evidence anchor: evidence_code_ref is .mcp.json (the concrete wrong-root mechanism behind failure mode A, fingerprint-able). The L1 gap itself is an absence in docs/loop-engine.md and is not fingerprint-able; accepted — grounding checks track the symptom, the architectural gap is carried by this description.

description:
M2 single-writer gate (commit 37a79e0 + .gitignore:47-49 + the rm --cached in 171cd6e) groups 3 registry files — meta-state.jsonl, file-index.jsonl, runtime-state.jsonl — under one "uncommit from worktree" rule, but they have heterogeneous write profiles that were not debated per-file:
(1) meta-state.jsonl = operator-confirmed authority writes (MCP tools: meta_state_report/resolve/batch/log_change) + minor hook-append noise (MCP-connection-failure, recurrence). NOT auto-generated.
(2) file-index.jsonl = auto-regenerable derived artifact (refresh_file_index / seed-file-index recomputes it from the tree; plan 260704-0301 rule 3: "rebuilt post-merge, never merged").
(3) runtime-state.jsonl = runtime ledger (gate overrides, budget states, dispatch ledger rows) — session/runtime state.
Grouping them conflates deliberate authority writes with regenerable/runtime state. Two risks to debate carefully:
A. meta-state.jsonl arguably should be EXCLUDED from the gate (committable normally, not gitignored) since its writes are operator-confirmed, not auto-generated. Keeping it gitignored forces `git add -f` for deliberate migration commits and re-stale's the .gitignore once it is force-tracked (current state on branch lifecycle-status-stale-mechanism: meta-state.jsonl is tracked AND still listed in .gitignore).
B. Each uncommitted file must have a SINGLE consumer dependency; if it is consumed in multiple code paths, uncommitting it risks silently disabling those consumers. file-index.jsonl is consumed by grounding (checkGrounding/cold-tier-regression/query-drift/derive-status); runtime-state.jsonl by the constraint gate (readRuntimeObservations) + runtime_state_read + budget checks. All 3 files are tracked on main; merging this branch removes file-index.jsonl + runtime-state.jsonl from main's tree, with NO automated post-merge regeneration (no CI step runs seed-file-index.mjs; no hook). Verified: with the files absent, readFileIndex returns an empty Map and readRuntimeObservations fail-open returns [] — so main's CI stays GREEN but the loop's drift detection + constraint gate are silently disabled on fresh clones of main until a manual seed-file-index/refresh_file_index. This is a symmetric "remembered manual step with no automated guard" gap to the one M2 was built to fix — the gate closes the worktree-commit side but opens an equivalent gap on the main-post-merge side. Debate: (a) per-file gate rules instead of one grouped rule; (b) either automate post-merge file-index regeneration in CI (seed-file-index step) OR keep file-index.jsonl + runtime-state.jsonl tracked on main so consumers stay populated.

added:
**Closed loop and downstream enforcement:** the gap detector + session-start surfacing close the named un-block for the deferred SessionEnd/pre-commit hook. The detection surface is the set of bound-artifact prefixes whose edits should trigger a change-log (`CHANGE_LOG_BOUND_PATHS`); the surfacing reads the branch's touched paths via read-only `git` and joins them against change-log coverage derived from each entry's `change_target` + `applies_to.schemas` (canonicalized to absorb `#anchor` suffixes, the `learning-loop-mcp` → `learning-loop-mastra` rename, and bare loop-internal schemas). The result lands in `.claude/session-context.json` as `change_log_gap_hints` so the operator/agent sees "N bound edits on this branch have no change-log" and can backfill via `meta_state_log_change`.

The surfacing is advisory, not a gate — it cannot block edits or writes. Coarse prefix-descendant matching over-covers by design (a single `"docs/"` log silences all docs gaps); for an advisory signal, false-negative-safe beats noisy false positives. The detector intentionally leaves no persisted state; it derives the gap set per session and surfaces it. Recurrence ownership lives with the deferred SessionEnd/pre-commit hook, not here — that hook re-runs the detection at session end and keeps a per-(branch, path) gap counter so a *recurring* gap (drift rate above threshold) can be promoted into enforcement. The closed loop closes at the hook; this plan ships the detection the hook will call.

description: Inbound state gate re-fires and dumps the full stale-active-observation inventory into operator context on every message matching a broad state-change regex. `core/evaluate-inbound-gate.js:33-45` `buildContextMessage` joins ALL stale ids via `.map(o=>o.id).join(", ")` with no dedup, no limit, no surface-scope; lines 64-66 fire on every state-change regex match (STATE_CHANGE_PATTERNS line 14-24 matches common words: budget/resource/limit/exhausted/reset/replenished). Observed this session: ~44-line dump of `vnstock-device-slot-*` observations (with duplicates — same id repeated up to 8x) injected into a meta-surface/intake-chain session twice (the red-team turn and the following turn). Four symptoms: (1) re-fires every qualifying message — no once-per-session memoization; (2) no dedup — duplicate ids listed verbatim; (3) cross-surface noise — vnstock stale debt floods a meta/intake session, no surface scoping to the current task; (4) unbounded list that grows with stale-observation debt → O(messages x stale) context leak. Root cause: the gate conflates *detecting* a state-change signal with *surfacing* the full stale inventory inline. Distinct from meta-260616T0222Z-inbound-gate-js-still-contains-a-local-ttl-based-staleness-c (that is the duplicate TTL-detection mechanism; this is the emission/surfacing behavior). Fix direction (simplification cascade): emit a one-line scoped, deduped, rate-limited pointer — "N stale active observations detected (surfaces: vnstock); review via meta_state_list/runtime-state read; inline list suppressed (already surfaced this session)" — instead of the full inline dump. Collapses 44 lines + re-fire + dupes + cross-surface noise into one line per session. Open: should the pointer also gate on surface-relevance to the active task, or just dedup+rate-limit?
resolution: Fixed via PR #45 (commit fbbbccf, merged). Inbound-gate inline stale-observation dump collapsed to a deduped, surface-scoped (affected_system), rate-limited pointer. Repeat same-signature warnings within a 30-min window (SUPPRESS_WINDOW_MS) collapse to a one-line 'already surfaced' pointer; a changed stale set re-emits. .inbound-stale-surfaced suppress token wired in the hook. Change-logs: meta-260709T0512Z-tools-learning-loop-mastra-core-evaluate-inbound-gate-js + meta-260709T0512Z-tools-learning-loop-mastra-hooks-universal-inbound-gate-js. description: meta_state_batch MCP tool rejects valid batch operations with validation_failed: the MCP wire layer coerces array-typed fields (e.g. the `operations` array and nested `change_diff.added/removed/changed`) into {item:[...]} object form, which then fails the handler's JSON-Schema validation. Observed 2026-07-09 during PR #44/#45 merge-conflict resolution: an agent attempted meta_state_batch to apply a multi-op registry closeout and got validation_failed; the workaround was one meta_state_log_change per op. This blocks legitimate batch registry operations (the tool's stated purpose — 'Apply a batch of meta-state operations atomically', cap 500 ops) and forces N round-trips where 1 should suffice. Distinct from the merge-conflict process issue (that is about append-only EOF conflicts; this is a wire-format/schema bug in a specific tool). Fix direction: the per-kind patch schemas built by buildPatchSchemaForFrom core/meta-state.js must round-trip through the MCP wire layer without array→{item} coercion — likely the tool inputSchema needs `type: array` preserved at the wire boundary, or the handler needs to accept the coerced shape. Repro: call meta_state_batch with operations:[{op:"write", entry:{...}}] and observe validation_failed vs the same entry accepted by meta_state_report/log_change.
resolution: Fixed in commit 4b6402f ("fix(meta-state-batch): recursive envelope strip accepts wire-coerced ops"). Plan: plans/260709-1032-meta-state-batch-wire-fix/. Root cause: MCP wire layer coerced both top-level `operations` array AND nested array fields inside each entry (change_diff.added/removed/changed, loop-design.addresses, loop-design.proposed_design_for) into {item:[...]} form, which then failed metaStateEntrySchema.safeParse inside metaStateBatch. Fix: added deepStripEnvelope (recursive, fail-closed) to core/envelope-stripper.js; applied via z.preprocess on the operations schema AND in the handler as defense-in-depth for direct-call paths. Removed redundant per-field stripEnvelope on entry (DRY — deepStripEnvelope subsumes it). New tests cover top-level coercion, nested change_diff coercion, double envelope recursion, parity with meta_state_log_change, and fail-closed on non-envelope input. Test result: 1138/1138 legacy-mcp pass, 1375/1375 full suite pass (post-fix). Rec 12 change-log: meta-260709T1057Z-tools-learning-loop-mastra-tools-handlers-meta-state-batch-t.

description:
Parallel PRs that each commit append-only meta-state.jsonl change-logs IN-PR (the 'clean git revert' practice) will ALWAYS conflict at EOF when merged sequentially, because both branches append distinct lines at the same end-of-file position and git cannot auto-merge same-position adds. Observed 2026-07-09: PR #44 (meta-260709T0503Z-* entries) and PR #45 (meta-260709T0512Z-* entries) were cut from the same main base; #44 merged first; #45 then blocked on a meta-state.jsonl EOF conflict. A prior agent exhausted 4 resolution paths and declared BLOCKED (Write tool → write-gate; meta_state_batch → wire-format bug; meta_state_log_change → re-records a DUPLICATE entry meta-260709T0959Z-* then auto-classifier 'blanket bypass'; custom python merge → 'scope creep'). The WORKING path that was missed: git-native `git merge-file --union`, which is gate-allowed (gate_check ok) and unions both sides' append-only lines in place — resolved cleanly (266 lines, valid JSONL, all 4 entries). Resolution sequence that worked: (1) git checkout HEAD -- meta-state.jsonl to discard any uncommitted duplicate noise; (2) git merge origin/main --no-edit → conflict; (3) git checkout --ours meta-state.jsonl (clean ours, no markers); (4) git show :1: and :3: to /tmp; (5) git merge-file --union meta-state.jsonl /tmp/base /tmp/theirs; (6) git add + commit. Root cause + mitigations to debate: (a) record change-logs POST-merge on main instead of in-PR — eliminates the conflict entirely but loses the 'in-PR for clean revert' property and changes the Rec 12 trigger timing; (b) add `meta-state.jsonl merge=union` to .gitattributes so EOF append conflicts auto-resolve (matches the file's append-only semantics; the union driver keeps both sides' lines) — smallest fix, but .gitattributes is itself a bound-artifact change and the union driver keeps lines in indeterminate order; (c) sequence PRs that touch meta-state.jsonl (don't cut parallel branches both appending) — process discipline, no code. This finding is the evidence argument; the mechanism choice is a separate workstream (relates to the M2 single-writer-gate debate meta-260708T0355Z about whether meta-state.jsonl should be gitignored/committable).

Confirmed absent: no `.gitattributes` file; no `merge=union` config; no post-merge-logging process; no PR-sequencing shipped. Workaround (`git merge-file --union`) documented in finding's description, but the root mitigation is still the M2 single-writer-gate debate (`meta-260708T0355Z`).

description: docs/runtime-contract.md has a transport-agnostic contract gap between Capability 3 (record routing) and the shell-hook-only transport description. Capability 3 states: "The runtime never writes `records/**`, `meta-state.jsonl`, or `runtime-state.jsonl` directly. All writes go through the loop's tools, so the registry's invariants (4-kind union, status lifecycle, grounding fingerprints) hold regardless of which runtime produced the record." The shell-hook-only transport description states: "only the gate hooks are wired (no MCP tool surface); the runtime relies on the loop's file-based records **without a tool channel**." These two statements tension: if there is no tool channel, how do writes satisfy Capability 3? Two readings surfaced in plans/reports/from-root-cause-to-transport-decision-260711-0011-mcp-stateless-adapter-vs-cli-report.md §7: (a) shell-hook-only is read-only participation (capability 3 is unsatisfiable under that transport, contract should say so); (b) a CLI counts as a tool channel (just not MCP), so the contract should name it and wiring a CLI would *define* the shell-hook-only transport rather than merely wire it. Unresolved since plan 260711-0011 (Unresolved Question #2 in the prior decision report). This is a pre-existing L2 design question; the current plan 260711-0030 (stateless MCP adapter) does NOT wire shell-hook-only and explicitly defers the question. Decision needed before any non-MCP transport is wired (library-import or CLI). Deferred to follow-up plan per the prior report's recommendation. Scope: contract clarification + optional transport definition.

tools/learning-loop-mastra/__tests__/legacy-mcp/lifecycle-migration-finalize.test.js contained hard-coded snapshot count assertions tied to a specific migration event: `>= 22 open findings` (the migration flipped 10 active + 12 stale → open) and `>= 229 total entries` (the pre-migration registry size). Both broke under concurrent MCP-session activity (the test failed 2026-07-11 when 2 migrated findings got resolved by other sessions, dropping the open count from 22 to 21 — the underlying H7 cross-process race the stateless MCP adapter plan 260711-0030 addresses). Refactored the test to remove the redundant `>= 22` (covered by 0-active + 0-stale structural invariants) and the unrecoverable `>= 229` (no-losses cannot be expressed without migration markers). The deeper schema problem remains: future migrations cannot express structural post-migration invariants because the registry has no migration_target + pre_count + post_count fields on the migration's change-log entry. Proposed fix: when a migration runs a `meta_state_batch` that flips many entries at once, the migration should write a separate change-log entry with `applies_to.migration: { target: 'X', pre_count: {finding: 22, total: 229}, post_count: {finding: 22, total: 229}, idempotency: 'sha256-of-mutation' }`. Post-migration tests then assert: `meta_state_list({entry_kind: 'change-log', ref_by: 'migration-target-X'})` returns exactly the expected count; the migration's affected entries are tagged in their `change_diff.migration_target` field and can be enumerated for post-hoc structural checks. Today's finding is open; ships when the next migration lands.

`meta_state_patch` corrupts `entry_kind` on existing loop-design entries when given an empty or non-kind-specific patch. Repro: this session patched loop-design-migration-markers-on-change-log (entry_kind stored as 'loop-design') via `meta_state_patch({id, entry_kind: 'loop-design', patch: {}})` — the result was the entry's stored entry_kind flipped to 'finding' (and version bumped). Same bug hit loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper in the same session.

**Root cause:** the patch tool's `patch` param is `z.union(PATCH_KINDS.map(k => buildPatchSchemaFor(k)))` (meta-state-patch-tool.js:23). An empty patch `{}` validates against the FIRST union member (finding) because all 4 per-kind schemas are `.partial().strict()`. The finding schema has `entry_kind: z.literal('finding').default('finding')` (meta-state.js:89), so Zod's parse applies the default and emits `{entry_kind: 'finding'}`. Then `Object.assign(entry, cleanPatch)` at meta-state.js:646 writes that into the stored entry.

**Affected entries (corrupted this session):**
- loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper — stored entry_kind='finding', should be 'loop-design'
- loop-design-migration-markers-on-change-log — stored entry_kind='finding', should be 'loop-design'

loop-design-operation-envelope-on-change-log was created cleanly via propose_design and is unaffected.

**Blocker:** the patch tool is now also unable to FIX these entries because line 43 (`if (entry.entry_kind !== entry_kind)`) refuses when stored ≠ expected. Direct file writes to meta-state.jsonl are blocked by the write gate. The two corrupted loop-designs are functionally invisible to filters keyed on entry_kind (loop_describe's active_finding_count, meta_state_list with entry_kind filter, etc.).

**Recovery paths (operator decision):**
1. Operator-mediated direct file edit on meta-state.jsonl (requires operator preflight marker — gates normally block this).
2. New MCP tool `meta_state_repair_entry_kind` that explicitly patches entry_kind (gated, audited).
3. Fix the patch tool itself (prevent empty patches from writing defaults) and run a repair script that re-asserts entry_kind from the per-kind schemas' `.literal()` values.

**Recommended fix to the patch tool:** in `updateEntry` at meta-state.js:642-646, drop `entry_kind` from `cleanPatch` unless it was in the user's raw patch. The per-kind schema's `.default()` was a convenience that became a footgun under the union-of-parts patch schema.

**Relationship to the meta-pattern:** this is another instance of the silent-success / silent-corruption pattern captured in loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper. An `assertinvariant` wrapper around `updateEntry` that asserts `entry.entry_kind` is unchanged after the patch would catch this exact bug. Filing this finding as an instance of the assertinvariant meta-pattern.

title: assertinvariant: universal scope, every core-logic operation
description:
Canonical version of the assertinvariant primitive with universal scope (per operator direction this session). Scope is EVERY core-logic operation that owns an invariant the agent depends on — not a curated subset. Narrow scopes are hand-wavy and miss the next domain (this session proved it 3 times: narrow design 2 to migrations → broadened to 8 kinds; narrow v1 to write-tools+file-readers → broadened to include updateEntry/archiveEntry/deleteEntry after the patch-tool corruption bug hit; narrow patch-tool fix to drop entry_kind from cleanPatch → broadened to the universal identity-invariant wrapper).

**Why universal scope:** curating the call site list produces hand-wavy fixes that miss the next instance. This session is the proof — three narrow-scoped attempts, three misses. The primitive's cost is uniform; its benefit compounds with every call site. Operator direction: "if not universal, the agent will hand-wavy".

**Wrapper primitive:** `assertinvariant(operation, {accept, returnOnFail, logTo})` at `core/operation-invariant.js` (separate file, not co-located with meta-state.js — separation of concerns). Returns structured failure shape when violated.

**Seed call sites (5+ observed, universal scope means future sites are wrapped by default):**
1. `core/file-readers.js#L10` — unmapped active entries (finding #6)
2. `tools/handlers/meta-state-report-tool.js` — input id honoring (finding #3)
3. `hooks/universal/pre-commit` — consistency check stderr summary (finding #5)
4. `tools/handlers/meta-state-batch-tool.js` — operation envelope (design 3; finding #7)
5. `tools rm` consult-gate — import-chain analysis (finding #1)
6. **`core/meta-state.js#updateEntry`** — identity-invariant (this session's bug, finding meta-260712T0053Z-...)
7. **`core/meta-state.js#archiveEntry`** — identity-invariant (latent — same bug class)
8. **`core/meta-state.js#deleteEntry`** — identity-invariant (latent — same bug class)

**Companion rule:** `rule-assertinvariant-at-boundary` — agent-side consult that fires when a new core-logic operation is added. Fires on regex `export function (writeEntry|updateEntry|archiveEntry|deleteEntry|...)` matches. Without this rule, the universal scope still applies but the agent has to remember to wrap; with it, the loop reminds.

**Canonical replacement for:** loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper (corrupted this session, will be repaired by operator). When that design is repaired, this design flips to status=inactive via supersede.

description: .github/workflows/test.yml pins fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> with version resolved from package.json devDependencies.fallow (currently v2.102.0). Local fallow binary is v3.3.0. The two versions emit materially different `audit` verdicts for the same diff: v2.102.0 reports `ISSUES: 3, VERDICT: fail` for the 3 PR-#53 gate failures (registry-writes.js unused-files, REGISTRY_FILENAME unused-exports, 8-line × 3-pair duplication), while v3.3.0 against commit aaf9995 reports `dead_code_introduced: 0, duplication_introduced: 0` for the same fix and instead attributes 1 pre-existing handler complexity as `complexity_introduced: 1`. v3.3.0 also follows `#lib/*` npm-import aliases across the analysis-root boundary (it sees tools/lib/gate-logging.js exports as unused, which v2.102.0 ignores). Local `pnpm fallow:gate` is therefore not a faithful predictor of the CI gate — passing locally may still fail on CI (or vice versa). Fix: bump package.json devDependencies.fallow, fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> pin, and version-specific flags (--coverage path, --dupes-mode mild) to a v3.x release that matches the local binary; re-baseline baselines/fallow/*.json against the new analyzer since analysis rules differ between majors.
resolution: Aligned CI to v3.3.0 to match mise-fallow debug binary. Bumped devDependencies.fallow 2.102.0→3.3.0, fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> 7ec8073…→c9326d9… (v3.3.0 tag), and refreshed the resolve-comment block in test.yml. pnpm install regenerated pnpm-lock.yaml (74 lines); both binaries now report `fallow 3.3.0` verified. v3 reports 3 NEW INTRODUCED false-positives (1 unused-file + 2 unused-exports) that v2 missed: (a) __tests__/helpers/manifest-constants.cjs — added __tests__/helpers/**/*.cjs to .fallowrc.json dynamicallyLoaded (test files require it; fallow ignores *.test.cjs so the requires are invisible); (b) tools/lib/gate-logging.js appendGateLog + logToolCall — added to ignoreExports (re-exports of canonical tools/lib/gate-logging.js outside fallow root; #lib/* alias consumers are out of scope). Re-baselined dead-code/health/dupes against v3 with the suppressions active. Verified pnpm fallow:gate ✓ pass (45 changed files, 0 issues), pnpm test 1840/1840 pass, pnpm test:cold-session 11/11 pass, workflow-shape.test.js 17/17 pass (SHA-pin regex accepts new 40-char hex). Two soft warnings remain on local-only (do not gate-block): node_modules directory not found (v3 looks at a different path than v2 on monorepo layouts) and "duplication baseline has 7 entries but matched 0 current clone groups" (v3 --save-baseline writes project-wide dupes while audit --dupes-baseline scopes to --changed-since origin/main).

description: Fallow's `audit` is a heavyweight analyzer (multi-second wall-clock per CI run, requires coverage data, runs in v2.102.0 vs local v3.3.0 with different attribution — see meta-260712T1431Z-github-workflows-test-yml-pins-fallow-rs-fallow-sha-with-ve) that emits findings many of which a fast pre-flight lint pass could catch at a fraction of the cost. The current CI pipeline (.github/workflows/test.yml) jumps from `pnpm test` straight to `Fallow audit (PR gate)` with no syntactic/static-analysis gate in between, so trivial parse/lint/syntax errors consume the same wall-clock as structural dead-code or duplication findings. Add a linter (eslint + project rules, or a stripped subset of fallow's own rules) that runs in <1s and gates before fallow — shift simple-syntax findings to a cheap gate and reserve fallow for structural audits. Affects every PR's CI wall-clock and the agent's debug-loop cost when iterating on fallow findings that turned out to be syntax-level.

The agent runtime invokes fallow through MCP integrations but defaults to fallow's human-readable output, which is context-inefficient for LLM consumption. Per https://fallow.tools/docs/integrations/mcp/, MCP integrations should pass `--format json` and parse the structured verdict block (`verdict`, `attribution.dead_code_introduced`, `duplication_introduced`, `complexity_introduced`, `summary`) instead of running fallow and grepping the rendered report for `pass`/`fail` symbols. Human-readable fallow output (the verbose ✗/●/·/■ format) consumes several times more tokens than the equivalent JSON for the same findings, AND the runtime currently has to skim the entire output to extract a single decision. The runtime-side MCP integration should default to `--format json` and consume only the verdict + attribution fields. Verified empirically 2026-07-12: local `fallow audit --format json` returns the verdict + attribution block in ~3KB while the human-readable equivalent is ~30KB for the same diff.
resolution: Encoded as rule-fallow-brief-on-gate-failure (consult-checklist, 1 item). The originating finding's empirical claim about fallow output token cost was based on a 2026-07-12 measurement that does not match fallow 3.3.0 behavior on this codebase today. The right primitive is `--brief --format compact` — a one-line-per-finding CSV stream that replaces the gate's decorated human report. PROCESS_HINTS row #5 surfaces the hint at session start; cold-session parity test enforces mirror alignment. See plans/260714-1200-fallow-brief-discovery/reports/byte-size-measurements.md for Phase 1 measurement data.

description: The agent runtime executes `pnpm test` (and `pnpm test:cold-session`) then parses the human-readable node:test runner output by grepping for `✖` failure symbols across potentially hundreds of test names and assertion lines — this is context-expensive (each test name + assertion becomes token overhead) AND structurally wrong (re-running the full suite on each debug iteration re-floods the agent context with previously-seen passing tests). The test runner integration should expose a structured failure-summary endpoint (e.g., `node --test --test-reporter=json` piped through a small filter, or a custom `pnpm test:failures-only` script that prints only failing test ids + first-failure trace) so the agent can target its next action without re-reading passing tests. This is the symmetric problem to the fallow-MCP-format-json finding (meta-260712T1431Z-github-workflows-test-yml-pins-fallow-rs-fallow-sha-with-ve): both human-readable CLI outputs are inefficient when the consumer is an agent. Mitigation: a small wrapper script that runs the suite, captures failures, and emits a structured JSON `{ failing: [{ test, file, error }], total_pass, total_fail, duration_ms }` — the agent then iterates on failures only, never re-reading the passing-test backlog.
resolution:
Resolved by the Phase 2 atomic cutover (plans/260713-1625-vitest-migration-replace-node-test-c8, commit a6ab4a2 on plan/vitest-migration, follow-up 9b26d12 that tightened fallow baselines + R2 guard after the code-reviewer subagent flagged gaps). Two concrete improvements close the finding:

1. vitest's --reporter=json (configured in vitest.config.mjs) emits a single end-of-run document to .test-logs/vitest-results.json with numTotalTests + numFailedTests + testResults[].assertionResults[]. The agent (and the ck:cook workflow) can grep numFailedTests>0 and read only failing assertions. The structured-failure endpoint is the durable fix the finding asked for.

2. pnpm test is now 'vitest run && node tools/scripts/sanitize-coverage.mjs'. The hand-rolled 199-LoC namespaced runner (tools/scripts/run-pnpm-test-namespaced.mjs) is deleted. c8 is dropped from devDependencies. vitest 4.1.10 + @vitest/coverage-{v8,istanbul} added.

Phase 3 (post-cutover verification) is the registered follow-up; it owns the cold-session timing re-confirm, the 7 r2/ security test re-audit, the full fallow:gate sign-off, and the agent-context smoke test (grep .test-logs/vitest-results.json structured output without passing-test flood). Phase 4 (registry closeout) ships the loop-design.

description: Fallow CI gate passes via a 5-step recipe when 1+ findings block. Run on the SAME fallow major the CI pins (devDep `fallow` in package.json + SHA in .github/workflows/test.yml); mise-fallow and pnpm-fallow diverge on different majors, so debug with `which fallow` first to confirm which one CI runs. Step 1 — diagnose: `./node_modules/.bin/fallow audit --root <root> --gate new-only --changed-since origin/main --format json`; read `attribution.{dead_code_introduced,complexity_introduced,duplication_introduced}` and the `dead_code.unused_files`/`dead_code.unused_exports` arrays filtered to `introduced: true`. The verdict is `fail` iff any introduced counter > 0. Step 2 — classify each introduced finding: truly dead (zero references in repo) → DELETE; used via `#lib/*` package.json import alias → fallow can't follow aliases that resolve outside its `--root`, so suppress; used via `require()` from a `*.test.cjs` / `*.test.js` file → fallow ignores test files entirely so the requires are invisible, suppress; used via same-file throw/catch string-match (`throw new Foo(...)` + test `err.name === "Foo"`) → fallow only counts external consumers, suppress. Step 3 — apply suppression via tools/learning-loop-mastra/.fallowrc.json: for unused-files, add a glob to the top-level `dynamicallyLoaded` array (this marks the file as an entry point so its consumers are reachable); for unused-exports, add `{ "file": "<path>", "exports": ["<name>", ...] }` to `ignoreExports` (the schema is strict on properties — file + exports only, no `_comment` keys; see v3 schema at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fallow-rs/fallow/v3.3.0/schema.json `$defs.IgnoreExportRule`). The existing config has precedent for `core/meta-state.js` ignoring FILE_INDEX_FILENAME/getFileIndexPath/_resetFileIndexCacheForTests. Step 4 — regenerate baselines so the suppressed items are NOT carried as "accepted": `./node_modules/.bin/fallow dead-code --root <root> --save-baseline <root>/baselines/fallow/dead-code-baseline.json` (and same for `health` and `dupes`); saving with the suppressions active means future diffs that re-introduce these symbols will fail the gate, instead of being silently masked by stale baselines. Step 5 — verify: `pnpm fallow:gate` (or `pnpm test && pnpm fallow:gate`) must show `✓ No issues in N changed files`. Two v3-specific soft warnings are NOT gate-blocking and can be ignored: `WARN node_modules directory not found` (v3 looks at a different path on monorepo layouts) and `Warning: duplication baseline has N entries but matched 0 current clone groups` (v3 `--save-baseline` writes project-wide dupes while `audit --dupes-baseline` scopes to `--changed-since`). Pre-flight sanity checks: confirm `package.json devDependencies.fallow` and the `fallow-rs/fallow@<sha>` pin in test.yml are on the SAME major (gate-class divergence is exactly what this recipe was derived from); confirm `which fallow` reports the mise-fallow version the debug session uses.
resolution: Fallow CI gate now passes legitimately on v3.3.0. Steps applied in the unpushed commits that landed via PR #53 squash (c725b2c): (1) bumped fallow devDep + workflow SHA pin to v3.3.0 (14f8109); (2) suppressed 3 v3 false positives in tools/learning-loop-mastra/.fallowrc.json + re-baselined dead-code/health/dupes against v3.3.0 (3f41a67). Verified locally end-to-end: `pnpm test` -> 1840 pass / 0 fail; `pnpm fallow:gate` -> "✓ No issues in 46 changed files" audited against the PR base d4e9c8e (the exact diff CI scopes). mise-fallow and pnpm-fallow both report 3.3.0 (no major divergence). CI test check went green on PR #53 (test + registry-deltas + fallow all SUCCESS), PR merged. The 5-step recipe is proven and now codified in the suppressions/baselines, so future diffs that re-introduce these symbols will fail the gate instead of being masked. NOTE: the final merge blocker was a SEPARATE pnpm/action-setup@v6 self-installer lockfile crash (Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'integrity' in undefined), not fallow; fixed by pinning pnpm/action-setup@v5.0.0 by SHA in .github/workflows/test.yml — outside this finding's scope.

title: Vitest migration — replace node:test runner and c8 coverage with vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8
description: Deferred design (loop-design) for migrating the 223-test node:test codebase to vitest. Replaces the hand-rolled per-namespace runner (tools/scripts/run-pnpm-test-namespaced.mjs) and the c8 + sanitize-coverage.mjs Istanbul-postprocess pipeline (package.json:17) with vitest's native test runner + @vitest/coverage-v8 (native V8 coverage; identical JSON shape to Istanbul since vitest 3.2.0 per vitest.dev/guide/coverage). Closes meta-260712T0730Z-test-runner-pollutes-agent-context structurally: vitest's `--reporter=json` (or `--outputFile` for per-file JSON) emits a single end-of-run document with `numTotalTests`, `numFailedTests`, `testResults[].assertionResults[]`, so the agent can grep `numFailedTests>0` then read the assertion results without re-reading passing tests on every debug iteration. Three-phase cutover (per ck:predict Recommendation 3): (1) shadow-run vitest in `--dry-run` mode against the existing 223-test tree, enumerate which tests fail to import (esp. the 3-arg `test(name, opts, fn)` signature that vitest 4.x removed — agent-parity.test.cjs uses it); (2) dual-run mode — keep `pnpm test` as-is, add `pnpm test:vitest-shadow` that runs vitest in parallel and reports diff; (3) cutover — replace package.json:17 test script with `vitest run --coverage --reporter=json --outputFile=coverage/vitest-final.json` and delete `tools/scripts/{run-pnpm-test-namespaced.mjs,sanitize-coverage.mjs}` + drop `c8` (package.json:38) + add `@vitest/coverage-v8`. Migration risks captured from ck:predict: vitest has no documented node:test interop (per vitest.dev/guide/migration — covers Jest/Mocha/Vitest 4 only); 22 .test.cjs files require ESM/CommonJS dual-handling under package.json `"type":"module"`; vitest 4.x poolOptions flatten + maxThreads→maxWorkers + vi.restoreAllMocks semantics change + coverage.all/coverage.extensions removal; cold-session timing envelopes (1s/10s/30s) need re-derivation; 5 security-critical `r2/` tests need per-test re-audit post-migration. Severity hint: low (parked; structural improvement, not a bug). Predecessor: meta-260712T0730Z-test-runner-pollutes-agent-context. Triggered by ck:predict comparison 260713-1503. Recommended preceding plan: Path A (TAP wrapper + pnpm test:summary + extracted test-globs.mjs) — ship the agent-context fix without entangling it with a 1-PR-per-phase framework migration.
resolution: Repair registry corruption: entry_kind was incorrectly set to "finding" by an earlier meta_state_patch (commit 583d39a's entry_kind+status injection guard ships in v1 but the corruption pre-dated that). Resolving the corrupt finding so a fresh loop-design entry can be re-proposed with the correct entry_kind and surface the addresses_inverse for meta-260712T0730Z-test-runner-pollutes-agent-context.
